input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Deploy the pre-configured Snort inline IPS between the FortiGate and the internal switch (place Snort on the LAN side, between firewall and hosts) so it can automatically detect and block ICMP flood/flooding patterns in real time.", "Configure FortiGate egress and DoS protections: enforce egress filtering/policies that restrict or rate-limit ICMP from internal subnets, enable ICMP-specific rate-throttling/anti-DoS thresholds and per-source connection/packet-rate limits to prevent a single host from saturating the network.", "Apply host-level network hardening on the compromised Ubuntu host: restrict or remove packet-crafting capabilities (tools that create raw ICMP packets), enforce least-privilege for network-capable processes, and enable host firewall rules to rate-limit or drop outbound ICMP while preserving legitimate management needs.", "Enable network- and host-based telemetry and alerting: turn on detailed ICMP/flow logging on FortiGate and Snort, collect host netflow/conntrack statistics, and create alerts/dashboards to detect sudden spikes in ICMP or per-host packet rates so responders can act fast.", "Apply network segmentation and strict egress policies by VLAN/subnet: isolate sensitive systems and limit which segments can send ICMP to upstream networks; enforce segment-level egress controls to reduce blast radius of any single compromised host.", "Where supported on switching hardware, apply per-port ingress policing or rate-limiting for abnormal traffic patterns from endpoints (limit packet-per-second or bandwidth for ICMP on access ports) and enable port security features to constrain misbehaving hosts."], "chosen_mitigation": "Deploy the pre-configured Snort inline IPS between the FortiGate and the internal switch (place Snort on the LAN side, between firewall and hosts) so it can automatically detect and block ICMP flood/flooding patterns in real time.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-12T16:15:05.705848"}

enforce segment-level egress controls to reduce blast radius of any single compromised host.", "Where supported on switching hardware, apply per-port ingress policing or rate-limiting for abnormal traffic patterns from endpoints (limit packet-per-second or bandwidth for ICMP on access ports) and enable port security features to constrain misbehaving hosts."], "chosen_mitigation": "Deploy the pre-configured Snort inline IPS between the FortiGate and the internal switch (place Snort on the LAN side, between firewall and hosts) so it can automatically detect and block ICMP flood/flooding patterns in real time.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-12T16:15:05.705848"}
